hero.name: Mohammed Akash
hero.intro: A freelance web developer and researcher who writes poetry now and then, mostly in Bengali — building in public with Claude, and writing it down day by day.

newsletter.heading: Get AI insights, code experiments & creative writing
newsletter.subtext: Weekly updates on tools, productivity, and building with Claude.

about.heading: A little about me
paragraphs: I'm Mohammed Akash — moh·akash, “the infinite sky.” I build for the web, chase research questions wherever they lead, and write poetry now and then — mostly in Bengali. I'm based in Bangladesh and work with people just about anywhere.

The foundation is freelance web development — around seven years of it now, for clients across roughly twenty countries, a five-star rating the whole way, and a few hundred projects behind me. That work taught me how to ship, how to listen, and how to make things people actually use.

Lately I'm pulling the other threads tighter. I published my first conference paper — a graph neural network for Banglish spell correction, at ICCIT 2025 — with a few more in progress, and I'm documenting what it's really like to build software in public with Claude, day by day. And I still scribble the odd poem, mostly to keep the other half of my brain awake.

facts.label: Background
facts.value: Freelance web developer, ~7 years and counting.

facts.label: Education
facts.value: BSc in Computer Science & Engineering, Bangladesh University of Business and Technology.

facts.label: Research
facts.value: NLP & machine learning — first paper at ICCIT 2025, on Banglish normalization.

facts.label: Writing
facts.value: Poems now and then — mostly Bengali — plus notes on building with AI.

facts.label: The goal
facts.value: To make honest things, stay curious, and understand the world a little better.

contact.heading: Let's make something under an open sky.
contact.intro: A project, a question, a poem you liked — the sky's open. I read everything and reply to most things within a day or two.
openTo: A kind word about a poem
